title: Glenn Hagel

infobox.value: [University of Manitoba](/wiki/University_of_Manitoba "University of Manitoba") [University of Regina](/wiki/University_of_Regina "University of Regina")

(born August 17, 1949[[1]](#cite_note-who-1)) is a Canadian provincial and municipal politician. He was a [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") member of the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 1986 to 2007, representing the constituencies of [Moose Jaw North](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_North "Moose Jaw North") or [Moose Jaw Palliser](/w/index.php?title=Moose_Jaw_Palliser&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moose Jaw Palliser (page does not exist)") at different times during his career.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He also served as [Speaker of the Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Speaker of the Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 1996 to 1999.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**Glenn Joseph Hagel** (born August 17, 1949[[1]](#cite_note-who-1)) is a Canadian provincial and municipal politician. He was a [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") member of the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 1986 to 2007, representing the constituencies of [Moose Jaw North](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_North "Moose Jaw North") or [Moose Jaw Palliser](/w/index.php?title=Moose_Jaw_Palliser&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moose Jaw Palliser (page does not exist)") at different times during his career.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He also served as [Speaker of the Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Speaker of the Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 1996 to 1999.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He was born in [Drumheller](/wiki/Drumheller "Drumheller"), [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta") and was educated at the [University of Manitoba](/wiki/University_of_Manitoba "University of Manitoba") and the [University of Regina](/wiki/University_of_Regina "University of Regina"). Before entering politics, Hagel worked as a counsellor, educator and coordinator for a number of organizations. He married Karen Gifco and they have two daughters, Kristin and Meredith, and three grandchildren, Juliet, Nora and Dexter.[[1]](#cite_note-who-1)

Hagel served in the Saskatchewan cabinet as Minister of Post-Secondary Education and Skills Training, from 1999 to 2001, as Minister of Social Services from 2001 to 2003, as Minister of Gaming from 2002 to 2003 and from 2006 to 2007, as Minister of Community Resources and Employment in 2003, as Provincial Secretary from 2006 to 2007 and as Minister of Culture, Youth and Recreation from 2006 to 2007.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Provincially, Hagel's career ended after being narrowly defeated by the Saskatchewan Party's Warren Michelson.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

He was elected mayor of [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw "Moose Jaw") in the [2009 Saskatchewan municipal elections](/wiki/2009_Saskatchewan_municipal_elections "2009 Saskatchewan municipal elections"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) and retired in 2012, deciding not to run for another term.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
